sentences.text: Fair.
Hello?
Hello?
Huh?
Yay!
How are you?
Good, how are you?
Good.
Happy Friday!
Yeah, it's, it's a Friday.
How's your guys' week gone?
It's been going.
It's been busy for a short week.
I feel like it has felt like a pretty standard week.
Yeah, no, I hear that.
I completely forgot it was a short week.
Yep.
You do anything fun for Labor Day, Colin?
No, we, we were pretty busy in the month of August.

We moved, we had our, you know, I had my internal marketing, like, our Avenue 5 marketing conference, and so we hit Labor Day, my girlfriend and I, and we were just like, let's, let's, let's take it easy, let's relax.
So we spent most, we spent the weekend kind of just Lounging around, finding local stuff to do.
Nice.
3-day weeks are the best.
I know.
be a real thing.
Agreed.
It has to be, like, a company-wide, or, like, an industry-wide, I mean.
It can't just be, like, one company that can't meet on Fridays.
We've all got to do it.
Exactly.
Nope.
Four-day work week over here.
Hmm.
Yeah, how was the marketing conference?
Oh.
-Oh, did we lose him?
Oh, sorry, what'd you… sorry, did you say anything?
Sorry.
No, you're good.
I said, how was the marketing conference?
It was good.
It was good.
Yeah, yeah, it was, we had a good time.
It's always nice.
It's one of my favorite things every year, getting to see, because you know, prior to COVID, I want to say, like.
90-95% of our marketing efforts were all, like, everyone was all based out of Phoenix, everybody.

And since COVID, it's just, like, been spread more and more thin, and so, you know, I would say that our marketing team in total, when you include our marketing operators, probably have about 75 people.
I mean, I only see, like.
maybe 8 or 9 of them on a weekly basis.
So, it's always awesome getting to see everybody, that, you know, you talk to them every day, but getting to see them in person is always nice.
And, you know, it's cool to have, kind of.

It's nice because it's, for me personally, it's nice to kind of gain perspective on what, each kind of arm of marketing is working on, and what they're working toward, and, you know, their initiatives and their goals, and… Because unfortunately, you know, because I'm not… we're not all sort of entrenched in the same place, it doesn't really, you know, it can get lost on you a little bit sometimes.
So, it was really good.
It was educational, and good to see everybody, so…
Nice.
Sounds fun.
I'm sad we couldn't be there, on the same week as our full company off-site.
Mmm…
So, I know, I went back and forth, and I'm like, can I skip my own company one to go to yours?
But next year.

I think… I think that was happening… I think that's a pretty common thing, though, because, like, we had one of our other suppliers, one of our digital marketing suppliers, she literally got off the plane from her companies.
So, I'm like, wow, okay, yeah, I guess, I guess everyone's having their summits right now, so…
Early August, it's after July 4th, kind of before fall for school season, just feels like it's the right time.
Sure does.
Tiana, where are you based out of again?
Are you in… are you in Phoenix?
No, I'm… I'm in Tucson.
Okay, Arizona still.
Okay.
Nice.
Anything interesting from the… you said, Colin, you got nice to see what other people are working on.
Any… any themes coming out of the conference?
You saw?
No, just sort of, better, you know, coordinating all of our marketing efforts, you know, the sort of, big picture stuff, but no, no, like.
trending issues or trending tasks or goals that, I picked up on.
I don't know about you, Tiana, but I didn't pick up on any of those.
Yeah, so I think our… from our NLC conference, as well as this one, the theme was kind of more just bringing more.

So we kind of discussed a little bit about that, and I think a lot of it… A lot of the sessions that we had were pretty foundational, so that way we could build upon those cornerstones to bring more to, you know, like, our obviously to each other, our company, our clients, and our on-site teams, and really how to be, you know, supportive in our roles.
And so I think that's kind of really, like, a consistent theme through throughout this year.
But yeah, there was definitely a lot of, you know, takeaways from it for me personally.
We, you know, got into, you know, client investment cycles and how marketing can participate in each of those stages, and where we can, you know, lean in and support.
And so I thought that was really awesome, and then also just, you know, being able to.

to kind of project manage, account manage, and… which kind of goes on both ends of not just our client side, but also with our, you know, our partnerships and our suppliers, like you guys, so I think it's… it was really helpful in a lot of areas.
I did want to mention, Maria didn't… messaged me, she says she is not able to join our call today, and Robert, who's now here, yay, his call ran A little bit over.
I cannot apologize enough for being late, I'm so sorry.
No worries.
Not a big deal.
That's, thanks for the context, Tiana.
That's, interesting what you said about getting involved in client, like, investing cycles.
That's, that's, smart and something I haven't heard from other of our partners talk about.
It's interesting.

Yeah, it is, and it was great because, they really… because all of us were marketers, so they really tailored it to, like, what is marketing's role in each of these stages, and, like, where can we, you know, really provide for our client the support they need, whether, you know, it's surveying the market to, you know, figuring out as far as closing up, you know, the you know, leasing and all that stuff, because of, you know, their focus is, like, they need to prepare for sale, right?
Or, you know, those kinds of decisions.
So it was very interesting.
I very much appreciated that session.
And of course, also Robert spoke as well.
Hi, Robert.
And there's a lot of data in there, which we all nerd out on.
Our team does.
This thing brings up, reminds me, do you all ever use ApartmentIQ?
Is that a product that's familiar to you?
our, performance team does use ApartmentIQ, yeah.
Okay.
Yeah, when you were talking about data, Tiana, and just competitive data, so ApartmentIQ, I don't know if you know this, Robert, it's the same company as Maven AI.
So…
Interesting, okay.
Yep, same company, just different products, so AV5 has been… Avenue 5 has been, customer of ApartmentIQ for almost a year now, I think?
So, where ApartmentIQ automates competitor data, we automate all things kind of local marketing on Google.
So, what you were saying, Tiana, made me think about if those… that's a tool you use.
If you don't have access to it, we can definitely get you access to it, but something that could help you gather comp data and support clients in their various stages of investment cycles.
Yeah, I don't know if our, like, our marketing operations team, utilizes it, but like Robert said, our performance team did, so…
Nice.
What was the topic that you spoke on, Robert?
I… I… so we have an internal BI tool called IQ5, and we were just launching a new, report in that.
Nice.

I was announcing that, and then showing the team what that's gonna look like, and… it was a little premature, but, we're… we're, you know, actually going to be launching it next week, so I'm super excited, and it's just a report that I feel It's gonna be super beneficial to our clients.
It shows some ROI data, as well as occupancy data.
Super high-level, monthly-type report.
Nice.
Sounds like some similar data to what IQ may be providing.
Yeah.
So, yeah.
I love that you have 5 behind every product, too.
We were talking to Sarah DeSantis.
Yesterday, she was talking about the Pop 5, which I had not heard of.
Which… is that familiar to you guys?
I'm assuming all… the PopFi, which is auditing ILS's websites, Google Business Profiles, and something else, two others that I couldn't remember.

Yeah, so it kind of focuses on a lot of just, you know, their website, and like you said, all their ILSs, and their, you know, just everything that really touches on, you know, their digital storefront, if you will, just to really make sure that, you know.

availability is correct, you know, and specials, and… and things like that, because we… and this is… we've had this for several years, when I was on marketing operations side, and I think it really helps us to be more proactive, in previous years.
There was unfortunate situations where clients came to us and was like, hey, this is not matching the website, like, what's going on here?

So, it really… Is, you know, we work with the on-site teams and our operations to make sure that we're ahead of that, and making those changes as necessary, so that way we don't have those situations again.
That makes complete sense.
You want to be the one that, if there's a discrepancy, you want to be the team that finds it, not your client.
Exactly.
sure that that is something that you're not missing, because, yeah, I'm sure clients can… be problematic if they find it more than once that something's wrong online, so… That makes sense.
Yeah.
Yep.

Well, a lot of the conversations we've had and having over the last couple weeks have been around, the growing importance of, like, AI overviews, and the play that Google business profiles have in that.
Is that something that your clients have been bringing up to you, or something that you guys have been talking with them about?

We've actually had, I think, more internal discussions about this, and so this was something that we talked about even at our conference, and it was… and we're actually trying to have these conversations with our clients ahead of time, right?
To, like, how can we make sure that, you know, ChatGPT and, Oh my gosh, the Google's one is escaping me right now.
is generating, you know, the property is generating, you know, in their recommendation list and in their summary.
So we have had lots of discussion about that.
Have you guys.
really been focusing on that as well.
I'm assuming you have, because, you know, your main thing is, is, you know, you give a Google Biz profile.
Yeah.

Yeah, yeah, we have, and we've been having a lot of discussions with customers around the… that's the importance of having your profiles fully filled out, is because Google's actually using that content to answer those AI overview questions.
So I was just curious, with you guys all getting together a couple weeks ago, if that was a hot topic, or while you're talking to clients, but sounds like it is.

It's been a hot topic on our end, and it's actually something we're… part of the reasons why I wanted to meet with you guys is we're building out, we're trying to see what internally we can do, and then also partner with the people who are kind of ahead of the game on that, so… We're… we're trying to… Catch up, and then also get ahead of our competitors.
Yep.
Don't think you're alone at all, Robert, in that.
Like, I think… there's still a lot of unknown with… are we calling… did we settle on GEO?
Do we call it that?
I…
We would pull…
at GEO, but I…
And the teams keep getting it confused with geofencing.
Yeah!
I'm like…
That's been, like… funny for me, but also frustrating.
I don't know what the… I feel like… I don't know what the industry's gonna… or the, you know.
what we're gonna land on with that terminology, but right now, I've been calling it GEO.
I think… so our CEO calls it… I say GEO, he says GEO, so I'm like, I think it's… I think you're supposed to enunciate the G there.
But I think we have landed on GEO, but everything that we're seeing on our end… one, there's a lot of unknown still, but it looks like the basics don't divert too much from SEO.
He's still, fresh, relevant.
unique content, and it appears that's what AI search agents also value, so I don't think it's too, too far off from SEO.
We were talking, like, we talked to Sarah yesterday, and she's like, we had a resident find us on ChatGPT.
And they're like, how do you know that?
Like, well, they kind of told us.
It's kind of hard to trace back if we were found on ChatGPT, so I think there's a lot of learning to be done there, but it appears, so far, a lot of the foundational SEO practices also apply to GEO.
G-Go, so.

We're really, as a product team, looking at ways to… what can we build like, specifically build instead of just give advice, but build to help with portfolios like yourself when you're… as you try to tackle and stay ahead of the game with GEO.
I've noticed ChatGBT is actually really good at making sure they have UTMs implemented, so I… I can see it in Google Analytics.
It's trending upward, we're getting more and more… it's not… it's not, like.
so much, where it's like, we need to jump on this, like, immediately, but it's… I can see it, you know, more and more every month.
We're getting a lot more referrals from ChatGBT, which is fascinating to me.
I will say some of the other AIs don't…

they don't have that implemented, so it's kind of harder to see, without having to, like, go through every single property and setting up a separate, channel for AI, which is probably… I'm hoping Google will do that automatically, but…

Google's gonna Google and probably take a while to set that up, and but yeah, it's… it's interesting how… a lot of the basics are SEO, and things that we should already have in place and already be doing.
I will say the things outside of SEO, which technically is still SEO, but, like.
There was an article that, Mario sent to us the other day that said Reddit was also big place that they get it from, and I'm like, I don't… don't make us go into Reddit, don't make us do that.
Which, I already know that, you know, Reddit is a big place for people to go and complain, and also ask questions and stuff, so I'm like, okay, I see the value in it, but don't make us do it.
And then the other one that threw me for a curveball was Wikipedia.
Really?
Yeah, I was like… And it's usually Wikipedia pages that have a lot of links in it, to kind of back up the page.
But yeah, Wikipedia is another really big, referral for, AI.
I heard Reddit, I did not hear Wikipedia.
Forgive my, novice question here, but how do you know via UTM code that ChatGPT use… was… it was using a ChatGPT result.
It's just, like, the… you could tell that the bot from ChatGPT, like, visited your website, or, like, how do you know that?
So, in Google Analytics.
they're.
through as, refer… Chat GPT slash referral.
And then in some of my testing, when I, when I, you know, am I… personally, I'm a religious user of ChatGPT, but I will click on a link, and then I'll actually look up at the URL, and I can.
Got it.
that it… they have one in place.
I'm like, they really did think of almost everything.
That makes sense, so the link click-off from ChatGPT specifically can be tracked back to it.
Okay.
Okay.
It's… it's interesting.
It's interesting.

Okay, I've taken us off the beaten path, but that's very, what you're thinking about is what we're thinking about, too, and I think We'll obviously keep you all updated as we continue to build out this product, but it's exciting times, like, I use ChatGPT all the time, and maybe we have to go back to Reddit, I don't know.
I used to be a big into Reddit, but I haven't… been a while.
when I… when I started in the industry, because I was new when I started, in Avenue, Avenue 5 four years ago, to the industry, I actually learned a lot from Reddit.
There were a lot of posts on Reddit.
Of course, LinkedIn was also a great place to find information in the industry, but Reddit was a surprisingly useful tool.
Nice.
We used to have an ILS part of our business, and we did paid Reddit ads.
So we would…
I'm looking.
ads.
paid.
we would put them in, like, the, in the threads about, like, where's the best place to live in Boston?
Like, the Boston apartment thread.
Yeah.
And we put, ads in there.
This is probably 2018, like, a long time ago, so… attribution was a little tough, but I'm sure they've got that figured out now.
I bet you could attribute that pretty easily.
Yeah.
That's great.

I was gonna say, we, you know, talking about kind of what goes into GEO and the value that, like, GEO provides and where they pull their information from, we were talking with, on one of our regularly, scheduled cadence calls we have with Sochi, you know, they'll bring to us a lot of times, they'll bring sort of industry insights and things that they're… teams have heard, and through talking to Google and other, individuals within the industry, and Something that, our account manager mentioned the other day is that they had learned from Google, actually, that, or at least one of their reps with Google that they have directly, that the way ChatGPT works, because OpenAI has a contract with Microsoft.
it's kind of interesting, so they'll pull Google, like, location, like, business location from Google, they'll pull, like, you know, amenities, reviews, things people have to say from Google.
But a lot of the basic content, so, like, the phone number, and the website, and things like that, are actually being pulled from Bing, because of that contract they have with Microsoft.
So, there's kind of this weird, in terms of, like.
basic level sort of contact information that is pulled into GEO.

There's kind of this weird blend right now of Google and also Bing, so, which… I don't love, because I, you know, I just don't think the juice is worth the squeeze in managing your own Bing listings and really getting in there and getting into the weeds with it, but That is something interesting that they brought to our attention, so I'm not sure how that's gonna… You know, move going forward, but yeah.
I had not heard that.
I hadn't either.
They have a… they have a listings product, so that would be a reason they want to bring it up, because they can help you keep your name, address, and phone number correct on Bing.
But it makes sense, because I know OpenAI has that, that, Microsoft partnership.
I heard that, that's interesting.
Are you guys using Sochi for the Google posts still?
I know when I first had met with Ali and Sherry when we started talking about this, I believe some properties were using it for the social side.
Are you using them for the Google side for properties still?
So they should be using it, and this could be a question for Corey, since she manages our social media, but, they are able to post from the social apparatus within Sochi to Google as well.
So, it's as easy as… yeah, it's as easy as, like, forming the, you know, they get in, they schedule their posts, they add the content they want to add, their hashtags, their keywords.
And then there's just 3 boxes at the top, and it's the connected networks, right?
So there's the Facebook, the Instagram, and the Google.
All you gotta do is click the Google one, and it'll publish to updates as well, so… I believe they should be doing it.
I'm not sure if they are.
Again, that's a Corey question, but, the capability is there.
Yeah.
Yeah, it's not one of our KPIs, and in our trainings, we do tell the teams that this isn't, like, a social site.
We really have them reserve it for highlighting, leasing specials, you know, office closures, open houses.

you know, amenity highlights, things like that, that you guys obviously already do, with Maven, but, not all of our site teams, like, post about those kinds of things, so, they, they do primarily use it for Instagram and and Facebook, but like Colin said, there is a capability.
It's just not something that we require.
Our properties do, but it's a… capability.

Yeah, I think customers that we have that either still use Sochi in tandem with us, or switched over to us, really it's because of the pushing of amenity keywords over and over, the pricing and availability, so just other features that, come with our optimization product over theirs, but yeah, I would… we do have an automated social tool as well that, I would love to show Corey, if she's open to seeing it.
Similar concept to the posts on Google, where we're feeding directly from your syndicate feed, to post real, live, amenities to your pages.
So it's all automated, takes out the work of creating the content, scheduling the content, automatically posts on your behalf.

When I saw Maria at your conference, at the, this summer, we talked for a sec, and she said, I've yet to see an automated social product that I'm impressed with, so we had, talked about getting time with her, too, to just show her what we're up to.
So it sounds like she's looked at some.
Hasn't been super impressed, so we'll keep, working with her, too.
Talk to her as well.
Cool.
Well, we did put, a deck together with some information.

I know the three of you have seen a demo already, obviously, so I don't need to go through all of those, but, a couple things that I did want to highlight, one being that we just hit a pretty big milestone, so, We just hit 2 million, units on with Maven AI.
So, I think it just kind of speaks to, how important it is to maximize visibility and performance across your Google profiles, and a lot of companies have started partnering with us to do just that.
It's the right place, right time.
A lot of folks, like you all, Rob, your team knows, Google Business Profiles are important.
It's hard to do… it's hard to do manually.
This product solves that, and so we've seen just a ton of adoption in the industry as… as… AI is growing, and then the importance of Google and getting relevant content continues to grow as well.
So, happy to partner with you guys to be a part of those 2 million units, and excited to keep, kind of, keep growing.
I'll jump to, the current partnership.
So, we have 25 properties on now.
So that was one of the reasons that I'd emailed you, I think, last week, Robert, was we've had, different people from different teams reaching out to add properties, which is great.

And usually when we get about this point, we, like to connect, because there's some easier onboarding options here, and we have a way to track all properties that are live, and a little bit more of a streamlined process to get properties live.
So, that is something I would love to chat with you guys about, but I know that today you had asked to go over reporting.
And where we're at with reporting for those first… it was the first 5 properties that we went live.
So, they've just hit the first 90 days of being live with us, so we do have some reporting to share.
Alright, so, reporting-wise, this, tool, it can be pretty customized, kind of just based on how you guys want this data to be sent over.
But we have it pulled two different ways.
So, this first report, and this is just showing the five properties that joined in, like, mid-May.
So we have at least 30 days worth of data now, or 90 days worth of data to show you.

So, the overall performance is going to be showing you an average of all 5 of those properties, and the change that you're seeing, overall to those in Google Analytics, we're pulling directly from your Google Analytics into this report.
So, if you were to go into GA4 and put these same date ranges in, you should see the same numbers.
So that'll be what's up at the top.
And then the dates we're comparing for this is going to be the last 4 months compared to the prior 4 months.
Prior to…
Last… that was last year, prior…
This one's year over year, yep.
This one's year-over-year, okay.
Yeah.
That would be the other one, then.
So this one's gonna be the last 4 compared to the same four last year to match seasonality.
Do you want to scroll down, Kylie, just to get my There you go.
This is gonna break down search impressions and maps impressions by those 5 properties, and just the increase we're seeing in the same date range.
And then we also like to include search and map impressions by device.

This one's not super, out of the norm to see that most of the impressions are coming from mobile, so we like to put this one in just to remind everybody that making sure their profiles and their property websites are very mobile-friendly is going to be super important, as more renters are searching that way.
And then the very bottom breaks it down by property.
So that's just going to show you all the same KPIs you saw up at the top, but, broken down by each individual property, so you can see those changes as well.
So there's obviously one big outlier in here, the Brentwood.
This is, I believe because it's a new-build property.
Yeah, I was gonna say, I'm pretty sure that one was a lease up when we got it, so that makes sense for that to be.
For Lenox SoCo,
The… the dip in website actions.
And… onward from there.
Is that typical, would you guys say?
I think it depends.
It can be a little hard to say, because these reports are, of course.
they're bringing in, like, overall data based not only on what we're doing, but just what you guys are doing overall on Google and with your SEO.
So it could be that there was more competition that was in the market during that time period, or it could just be that this property needs a little bit more time.
So…
Okay.
I think typically, once we see, like, a 9-12 month report, you're not really seeing these numbers in the red anymore, but we're just 4 months into that.
Okay.
We'll share reporting at 90 days.
We always tell our customers, like, hey, this is… if you could move Google in 90 days, everybody doing it.
It just takes time.
And so, we tend to see some fluctuations in the first 90, 180 days, and then for all of our customers that have been on with us for a year or two, three years, it's just always it's always green.
It always is a lift, and then, like Morgan said, Lenox, SoCo, there's so many factors in there, but I'd expect those numbers to continue to go up and be positive year over year.

Yep, and we also pulled the same report, but like I said, the last 4 months compared to the previous 4, so that one will be a little more accurate just in terms of not pulling in the high data from, like, a new build property.

So, different ways that we can look at these reports, just so you guys know, you know, you can let us know what kind of, dates you would want us to pull, we can auto-schedule these, I can also export them into a PDF and then send those over, so based on how you want these to look and how often you need them, I can set that up.
what are the key, like, KPIs that you're typically tracking with Google?
Like, how are you measuring success with Google for other properties that, maybe are doing less posts?
That's a really good question.
We are… we don't have KPIs for those quite yet.
unless Colin Tiana, correct me if I'm wrong there, that's… that's something we're… Kind of working towards in that direction.
And trying to… trying to figure out what those are, since we… Haven't had those in the past.
Yeah, we don't have anything for those yet, but like Robert said, it's on the horizon.
We'd like to, you know, kind of start tracking that information.
Setting benchmarks.
Yeah.
Well, we can share both of these reports, so you can get a PDF of both of them.
And then I know we have other properties on outside of these.

We just pulled these, because these are the ones that have been on the longest, and like we were just explaining, it does take some time to show an increase in that, so as we have more time, we'll have more reports to be able to share and chat through.
And these are all Texas properties, right?
Yeah.
Yeah, I think so.
Boston, Austin, Austin.
Yeah, and Lord knows Texas is…
Yeah.
Texas right now, so it'll be interesting to see some of the other… the other regions and how they perform.
Yeah.
Yeah.
I feel like Texas is so unstable right now that it's… It's hard to… hard to tell there.
Yep.
Do you guys have benchmarks?
Just kind of speaking of, does Maimon AI have… have benchmarks that they… Like, either your own or, like, industry.
Or regional, I don't know.
Kyle, if you go back to the… the deck, and go to the what we heard slide.
Yep.
So, yes and no, Tiana.
The main reason companies buy this product is, and this is what we heard from your team, too.
Is the first bullet point.
Google's important, on-site team members, they're not getting to it.
They're not updating their profiles.
If they are, it's time-consuming, so it's not standard across the portfolio.
So, one of the main metrics we'll share is the amount of content that's being created, and the hours that are saved, like, if you tried to do it this much manually.
So we always talk about that.
And then the… the lift in traffic is like a lagging indicator.
Because of all this.
content on your profile, you're saving time, you're becoming more efficient, and then, by the way, we also see a lift in traffic.
We never promise anything over 100%, but we've seen, in general, year over year.
The median is, like, 30, 40, 50% lift in all of those metrics, year over year, is what we typically… Yeah.
huh?
We've seen more, like, a lot more, but we never… we try not to promise it, because, like, what Colin said, or sorry, what Robert said, Lord knows it's Texas.
There can be variance in… in which markets.
Right.
Yeah, no, absolutely, I get that.
Yeah.
Thank you.
I don't know if that helps answer your question, but…
It does, yeah, we have… we had similar conversations.
We're like, we can't guarantee this, but what we've seen… Has been, you know, XYZ.
So yeah, I get it, and I appreciate your transparency there.
Yeah.
Well, we do have one new feature that was just rolled out for your properties that I wanted to share while we're on.
So, obviously, just a reminder of the areas throughout the profile that we are automating.
It's the daily posts.
The live pricing and availability feed and the products feed, concessions being pulled directly from your feed to your profile, and Q&A.
We have launched amenities in the products feed.
So, the products feed being where the pricing and availability feed lies.
We now have community amenities and unit amenities rolled out for your properties.
I was so excited when you guys said that, it looked so good.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah, we're very excited about it as well.
I think it looks so nice.
